$comment: c91 (D-CSUBSET-ARRAY-DECAY-POINTER-IDENTITY + D-CSUBSET-ARRAY-DECAY-IN-COMPARISON + D-CSUBSET-ARRAY-DECAY-IN-CONDITION, 2026-07-02): an ARRAY in a value position where a POINTER is expected decays to &arr[0] (C 6.3.2.1p3) — comparison operands both orders (==/!=/</>), condition positions (if/&&/ternary/!), and the identity law arr == &arr[0] == &arr. Pre-fix a MEMBER/GLOBAL array operand was VALUE-LOADED (the compare read the array's first BYTES as a pointer): sqlite sqlite3ParserFinalize `yystack != yystk0` always-unequal -> freed the on-stack parser -> `free(): invalid pointer` SIGABRT on every SQL statement (check 1 = that exact shape); a zero-filled global compared EQUAL to null (checks 8/9 = the zero-luck shapes). Locals were already correct (c63) — every must-fail-pre-fix operand here is member/global/deref. 19 checks, distinct exits, volatile-laundered pointers so no pass folds the compares. RED-ON-DISABLE: HIR comparison arm -> check 12 loud (A_); HIR condition arm -> checks 13/14/16 loud (I_); `!` arm + MIR c63-twin backstop -> the tier-local unit pins (the two tiers overlap by design). => 42.
